linkedinFeatured.heading: Featured Posts
linkedinFeatured.subtitle: Thoughts on UI, design systems, and mentorship — straight from LinkedIn. Follow along for more.

intro.heading: What people say
intro.subtitle: Feedback from product managers, designers, and engineers I've shipped with directly.

items.name: Priya Nair
items.quote: Jaymin turned a vague accessibility mandate into an actual, working component library in weeks. Every form we shipped after that audit just worked for keyboard and screen-reader users — no follow-up tickets.

items.name: Daniel Cho
items.quote: The board rebuild in React was the smoothest front-end migration I've been part of. Jaymin kept design and code in sync through Figma/Zeplin the whole way, so we never had that usual 'the build doesn't match the file' argument.

items.name: Meera Shah
items.quote: Most developers treat a dense data table as a styling problem. Jaymin treated it as an information-hierarchy problem, and the dashboard reads completely differently now — same data, actually scannable.

items.name: Arjun Mehta
items.quote: I learned more about CSS architecture and PR review from three months of pairing with Jaymin than from a year on my own. He explains the 'why' behind every review comment, not just the fix.

items.name: Sara Lindqvist
items.quote: The token-based component library cut our regression surface dramatically — once a pattern was fixed once, it was fixed everywhere it was used, not just in the one screen someone happened to notice.

items.name: Rohan Deshpande
items.quote: Quick turnaround on the landing page redesign, responsive across every device we tested.
